Scenario: continuous, two groups, d=.50, 3 repeated time points, exchangeable rho=.30
Note: App multiplies rounded independent two-sample n by DE=1+(m-1)rho, then rounds again. This is a planning approximation, not a full GEE package calculation.

Scenario: two-group repeated slope/change, d=.50, 3 time points, ICC/correlation=.30, power=.95
Note: App simple two-group LMM now uses longpower::diggle.linear.power; differences should be limited to rounding.
App uses the Schoenfeld event-based approximation, then rounds allocation-specific group sizes and sums them.

Scenario: two independent means, equivalence margin=.50 SD, true difference=0
Note: App mean equivalence now uses exact t-based TOSTER::power_t_TOST; differences should be limited to rounding.
epiR returns the maximum total N needed for sensitivity/specificity precision; this scenario is driven by sensitivity and matches the app sensitivity calculation.

Scenario: AUC=.70 vs null AUC=.50, case/control ratio=1
Note: App uses Hanley-McNeil AUC variance. Installed packages did not expose a direct one-AUC sample-size API for this scenario.

Scenario: two Poisson rates, rate1=1.0, rate2=1.5, allocation 1:1
Note: This is the same Wald rate approximation implemented by the app.

Scenario: parallel CRT, continuous d=.50, cluster size=20, ICC=.05
Note: App continuous parallel CRT now uses WebPower::wp.crt2arm and rounds raw total clusters to balanced clusters per group.
This is the same normal-approximation CI half-width formula implemented by the app.

Scenario: ICC precision, expected ICC=.70, 3 raters, half-width=.10
Note: ICC.Sample.Size targets ICC hypothesis-test power, while the app currently calculates CI-width precision.

Scenario: RMSEA close-fit test, H0 RMSEA=.05, H1 RMSEA=.08, df=50
Note: Both use MacCallum-style noncentral chi-square/RMSEA power; app rounds up.
